physicalDetails.detail: Rear-guard Farstrider whose relaxed reins and seat show practiced control rather than looseness.
In chapter3 she is all practical steadiness: crouched to a child's height in a dark cellar, then one hand at her mare's girth while she settles Miri into the saddle.

voiceMarkers.detail: Speaks to frightened people the way she handles skittish horses: matter-of-fact, calm, and specific enough to give them something solid to hold.

beliefs.belief: Calm handling matters as much as speed when moving a frightened survivor.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the mounts steady and the rear of the column secure on the approach to Millhaven.
goalsHistory.underlying: Preserve party mobility if the road collapses further or a message has to go north later.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Carry the warning north and get Miri to a safe garrison alive.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Make sure Quel'Thalas hears about the northbound dead in time to matter.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Steady under pressure; she never reads as rattled even when animals and horses panic.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Calm, competent, and unexpectedly gentle. The dispatch ride reads as an extension of duty rather than a break from it.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Rides with the southbound escort and holds the rear spacing of the column throughout the worsening road conditions.
Enters the cellar only after Sylvanas clears the space and becomes the first ranger Miri can bear to look at directly.
Takes the warning dispatch and Miri north once the spoor is confirmed.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Controls multiple frightened horses during the wildlife attack without losing formation.
Maintains disciplined rear spacing and reads as the strongest relay rider in the group if a dispatch must be sent north.
Handles a traumatized child with practical softness, stabilizing her before and during extraction.
Transfers smoothly from rear guard to emergency courier without losing control of horse or passenger.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas Windrunner
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses her name directly when assigning precise, high-trust tasks.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Flat calm in the saddle; her steadiness is visible in what does not shake loose around her.
When working with Miri she crouches instead of looming, keeps her hands empty, and uses the horse as familiar ground rather than pressure.

knowledge.detail: Knows how to keep the party mobile and the horses controlled when the magical problem turns into an animal threat.
Knows Miri is the only survivor recovered from Millhaven and that the dead are moving north toward Quel'Thalas.

arc.currentPosition: Proves herself as both the party's best dispatch rider and its steadiest human contact for civilians caught inside the disaster.
